title: Citrusy Flaky Dum Crumble
description: A nostalgic Korean dish featuring mint-infused dumplings with a light citrus crust that's both delightful and easy to prepare.

ingredients.name: Dumplings
ingredients.notes: Use low-fat dumplings for a healthier option.

ingredients.name: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: May be swapped with almond or coconut flour for additional flavor and texture.

ingredients.name: Coconut oil or melted butter
ingredients.notes: For a low-fat option, use liquid plant-based oil instead of coconut oil/butter.

ingredients.name: Mint leaves
ingredients.notes: Can be finely chopped for better distribution in the crust.

ingredients.name: Julienned citrus zest
ingredients.notes: Use zest from a single lemon or orange, finely grated to ensure even spread in the dish.

ingredients.name: Honey
ingredients.notes: Can be omitted for a more natural flavor profile if desired.

instructions: Mix all-purpose flour, coconut oil/butter, and a pinch of salt in a large bowl until well combined.
Fold in mint leaves and citrus zest into the flour mixture. The mixture should be crumbly but hold together when pressed.
Place dumplings evenly on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or silicone mat.
Spread the flaky dough mixture over each dumpling, ensuring an even layer covering half of the surface area.
Bake in preheated oven for 15 minutes, then reduce heat to 320°F (160°C) and bake for another 10-15 minutes or until golden brown on top and crisp around the edges.
Remove from oven and let cool slightly before serving. Garnish with extra mint leaves if desired.
